method_atom: An analytic treatment of the first-passage / escape statistics of an overdamped degree of freedom out of a metastable well, when it is driven by long-range temporally-correlated (power-law-spectrum, 'flicker') fluctuations rather than white noise. The correlated dynamics is written as a generalized Langevin equation and recast into an equivalent non-Markovian, time-convolutionless master equation, which is asymptotically solvable. The result is a power-law escape kinetics whose exponent depends exponentially on the ratio of the barrier height to the fluctuation strength, and which is qualitatively distinct from the prediction of the corresponding fractional Fokker-Planck reduction.
object_atom: Overdamped particles escaping a cusp-shaped parabolic potential well, driven by thermal fractional Gaussian noise with a 1/omega^(1-alpha) power spectrum at temperature T (subdiffusive Kramers-type escape).
brief_reason: The transferable part is the non-Markovian generalized-Langevin / time-convolutionless master-equation machinery yielding barrier-height-dependent power-law escape under power-law-correlated noise; the domain-specific object is overdamped particles thermally escaping a cusp-shaped potential barrier.
